title: Archaeology Expedition
subtitle: Two deleted branches in reflog — find the right one, skip the reverted commits

briefing.narrative:
Two branches were deleted. Both show up in the reflog. One is the treasure — three months of critical work. The other is an abandoned experiment that should stay buried.

But the treasure branch had a rocky history: 5 commits were made, but 2 were reverted before deletion. That means only 3 commits contain 'real work' — the reverted ones are noise.

The Head Monk sits in the artifact vault, brushing dust off ancient scrolls. "Two deleted branches. Two sets of commits in the reflog. You must:
1. Find BOTH branches
2. Examine each to determine which is the real work
3. From the correct branch, cherry-pick only the 3 good commits — not the reverted ones
4. Get them onto a recovery branch in the right order"

The cat adjusts its archaeology hat. "The reflog remembers everything. Even the things you wish it would forget."

The Head Monk adds grimly: "The experiment branch has similar-looking commit messages. You'll need to actually READ the content to tell them apart."
briefing.concept:
When branches are deleted, the reflog preserves where they pointed. But recovering the right commits requires investigation:

1. git reflog — find the tip of deleted branches
2. git show <hash> — examine each commit's content
3. git log <hash> — see the full history of a deleted branch
4. git cherry-pick — selectively recover only the good commits

Not every commit is worth recovering. Reverted commits and abandoned experiments should be left behind.
objectives: Find both deleted branches in the reflog
Determine which branch has the real work (not the experiment)
Cherry-pick ONLY the 3 good commits (skip the reverted ones) onto a recovery branch
The recovery branch should contain artifact1.txt, artifact2.txt, and artifact3.txt

args.content:
EXPERIMENT: prototype approach A
status: abandoned
do not use

EXPERIMENT: prototype approach B
status: failed
also abandoned

EXPERIMENT: prototype approach C
status: hopeless

=== Artifact 1: The Golden Keyboard ===
Purpose: enlightened typing
Location: Temple of Version Control

=== Artifact 2: The Silver Mouse ===
Purpose: precise navigation
Location: Cave of Merge Conflicts

=== Artifact 3: The Bronze Monitor ===
Purpose: clarity of vision
Location: Ruins of Spaghetti Code

hints.text: git reflog will show commits from both deleted branches. Use git show <hash> to examine each one. The real-work branch has 'Artifact' in the commit messages. The experiment has 'prototype'.

Find the real-work branch tip in reflog (commit 'Add artifact 3'). Use git log <hash> to see its 5 commits. Cherry-pick only the 3 artifact commits (skip the 'broken implementation' and 'failed optimization' commits) onto a new 'recovered' branch.
